title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people discussing their attitudes toward money, spending, and saving. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.D:
### Hannah, 32, Nurse
I’ve always been careful with money. [43]I keep a budget and try to save a little each month[/43]. That doesn’t mean I never treat myself, but [47]I think twice before making big purchases[/47]. [50]I believe it’s important to have some savings for emergencies[/50].
texts.C:
### Sam, 28, Graphic Designer
[44]I used to spend money as soon as I got it, but now I’m more responsible[/44]. I set goals for what I want to buy and [48]avoid impulse shopping[/48]. [51]I’ve learned that experiences are often more valuable than things[/51].
texts.A:
### Olivia, 40, Teacher
[45]Growing up, my family didn’t have much, so I learned to appreciate what I had[/45]. Now, I try to [49]find bargains and make my money go further[/49]. [52]I enjoy giving thoughtful gifts rather than expensive ones[/52].
texts.B:
### Jake, 36, Engineer
I’m not a big spender, but [46]I do like to splash out on holidays or special occasions[/46]. [47]I think money should be enjoyed, not just saved[/47]. [49]I try to balance saving with enjoying life[/49].

questions.question: regularly puts money aside for the future?
questions.explanation: Hannah keeps a budget and saves each month.

questions.question: has changed their attitude towards spending?
questions.explanation: Sam used to spend money immediately but is now more responsible.

questions.question: learned to value what they had due to limited resources?
questions.explanation: Olivia learned to appreciate what she had growing up with little money.

questions.question: enjoys spending money on special occasions?
questions.explanation: Jake likes to splash out on holidays or special occasions.

questions.question: is cautious about making expensive purchases?
questions.explanation: Hannah thinks twice before making big purchases.

questions.question: tries to avoid buying things on impulse?
looks for ways to make their money go further?
questions.explanation: Olivia finds bargains and makes her money go further.

questions.question: believes in having savings for unexpected events?
questions.explanation: Hannah believes it’s important to have savings for emergencies.

questions.question: values experiences over possessions?
questions.explanation: Sam has learned experiences are more valuable than things.

questions.question: prefers giving meaningful gifts rather than expensive ones?
questions.explanation: Olivia enjoys giving thoughtful gifts rather than expensive ones.
